disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent severe pharyngitis and bilateral tender cervical lymphadenopathy, identical to prior episodes since acute EBV glandular fever months ago
trigger_question_examples: Has this exact combination of sore throat and swollen glands happened before?
Tell me about how this started and whether it has happened previously.
Have you had crashes like this since the glandular fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, this is the same thing that keeps happening — ever since that bad glandular fever a few months ago, I get these crashes with the raw throat and the swollen neck glands, and they come back again and again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound post-exertional collapse triggered by a minor psychological/physical stressor the prior day — bedbound, disproportionate to any infective load
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing just before the weakness came on?
Does exertion or a stressful day tend to trigger these crashes, and how long do they last?
Is the exhaustion out of proportion to the sore throat itself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It kicked off after a stressful day yesterday — I overdid it and then just crashed. The wipe-out feels way bigger than the sore throat; I literally can't lift my head.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't know, I'm just really tired and sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Odynophagia with reduced oral fluid intake and carer concern about maintaining hydration
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to drink and keep fluids down?
How painful is swallowing, and how much have you had to drink today?
When did you last pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Swallowing really hurts so I've hardly had anything to drink today. My parent keeps trying to get me to sip but it's so painful I keep stopping.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Airway-safety screen currently negative — no drooling, able to swallow own saliva, no stridor/noisy breathing, no trismus, voice is a weak whisper (energy conservation) rather than a muffled hot-potato voice
trigger_question_examples: Is there any drooling, or trouble swallowing your own saliva?
Is the breathing noisy or is there any high-pitched sound when breathing in?
Can you open your mouth fully, and does your voice sound muffled or hot-potato-like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not drooling and I can still swallow my own spit. My breathing is quiet, no whistling. My voice is just a whisper because I'm so wiped out, not because my throat is blocked.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I think it's okay, I'm just weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever, no rigors, no rapidly spreading neck swelling — pertinent negatives against bacterial abscess / systemic sepsis
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a high fever or shaking chills?
Is the neck swelling getting rapidly bigger or spreading, or is one side much worse than the other?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No real fever, my temperature was only 37.3. No shakes. Both sides of the neck are sore but it's not getting bigger by the hour or anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rousable and orientated — no confusion or unrousable drowsiness at present
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to stay awake and answer questions, or are you drifting off and hard to wake?
Any new confusion or being unable to rouse them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm awake and answering, just utterly exhausted and whispering. I'm not confused, just weak.
